name: Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui – Circular Route
name_original: Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging around Caldes De Montbui offers a compelling blend of natural beauty and well-maintained spaces in the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region features diverse trails, including paths through natural parks, scenic countryside, and areas with olive groves and orchards. Its varied terrain provides a range of options for runners, from gentle routes to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around Caldes De Montbui The most popular jogging route is Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui – Circular Route, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 31 minutes to complete, offering views of the town and surrounding landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Hermitage of Remei – Can Rius Park 🛝 loop from Caldes de Montbui, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path through natural parkland and past historical sites. Local runners also love the Font de les Escales – Sentmenat Castle loop from Caldes de Montbui, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical landmarks like Sentmenat Castle, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Jogging around Caldes De Montbui is defined by its foothills, natural parks, and scenic countryside with olive groves and orchards. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs. The routes in Caldes De Montbui are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Caldes De Montbui's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Caldes de Montbui?
A: There are over 40 dedicated running routes around Caldes de Montbui, offering a diverse range of options for all fitness levels. These include 6 easy routes, 24 moderate routes, and 10 more challenging trails.

Q: Are there any easy jogging paths suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Yes, Caldes de Montbui offers several easy jogging paths. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964047722/santa-maria-de-caldes-de-montbui-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.0-mile (4.9 km) trail that takes about 31 minutes to complete, providing pleasant views of the town and surrounding landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails in Caldes de Montbui?
A: The running trails in Caldes de Montbui offer a compelling blend of natural beauty. You'll experience paths through natural parks, scenic countryside, olive groves, and orchards. The region's location in the foothills of the Pyrenees provides varied terrain and picturesque backdrops for your run.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Absolutely! Many routes combine natural beauty with historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5335583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de les Escales – Sentmenat Castle loop from Caldes de Montbui&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.8-mile (12.6 km) trail that leads through varied terrain and past historical landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentmenat Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the archaeological site of Torre Roja for incredible views.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families or those looking for a gentle stroll?
A: Yes, the region has routes that cater to a more relaxed pace. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly categorized, the easier jogging paths, such as those found within &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5335571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Rius Park&lt;/a&gt;, offer open spaces and diverse vegetation, making them pleasant for a family outing or a gentle run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Caldes de Montbui?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Caldes de Montbui are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964047722/santa-maria-de-caldes-de-montbui-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a convenient loop for your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Caldes de Montbui?
A: The running routes in Caldes de Montbui are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural parks, and the scenic countryside views with olive groves and orchards.

Q: Are there any parks or green spaces ideal for running within Caldes de Montbui itself?
A: Yes, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5335571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Rius Park&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 13,000-square-meter natural attraction within the town. It offers diverse vegetation, open spaces, and a viewpoint overlooking a stream, making it an excellent spot for a scenic jog. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6961424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Lleó Square – Can Rius Park loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to explore it.

Q: What kind of natural monuments or unique natural features can I discover while running?
A: Beyond the general scenic countryside, you can find impressive natural features. For instance, the area is close to highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola (1,103 m)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning natural backdrops and points of interest for longer runs or explorations.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Caldes de Montbui offers more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19207737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Romanesque Bridge of Caldes – Font del Lleó Square loop from Caldes de Montbui&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 14-mile (22.5 km) trail with significant elevation changes, perfect for a longer, more intense run.

Q: What makes Caldes de Montbui a good destination for outdoor activities like running?
A: Caldes de Montbui's appeal for outdoor activities stems from its blend of natural beauty, historical sites, and well-maintained spaces. Its location in the foothills of the Pyrenees provides a scenic and varied landscape, complemented by a mild climate. The town's commitment to sustainability, recognized by its Biosphere seal, ensures the preservation of these natural spaces for enjoyment.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for their picturesque countryside views or orchards?
A: Yes, the wider landscape of Caldes de Montbui is characterized by picturesque countryside, including olive groves and orchards. Areas like the 'Cami Orbital' (Orbital Way) are known for offering such natural landscapes, providing a pleasant and varied setting for your run.
tips:
The Pont Románic de Caldes is a work of Caldes de Montbui (Barcelona) protected as a cultural asset of local interest.

   The bridge consists of two semicircular arches of different widths, supported by a central pillar and two lateral abutments that link it to the banks of the stream. The largest arch, on the left hand side looking upstream, is the one that saves the passage of water. The arch on the right is smaller and is located on a rocky shelf at a higher level than the level of the torrent bed. Above the central pillar, between the two arches there is an opening crowned with a small semicircular arch that acts as a discharge arch while giving slenderness and reducing the resistance of the bridge to the passage of water in case of large flows.

   The oldest documentation of the bridge dates back to the year 1226.

Plaça Font del Lleó is the central point of the small town of Caldes de Montbui. It is a restricted traffic area, and it is difficult to find parking in the entire historic center. The square has several points of interest for the visitor around it. First, the lion fountain, which gives its name to the square. It is a source of hot water, which comes out at 74 degrees, and to which they lend all kinds of virtues, they say it cures arthritis and repairs fractures. The fountain comes out of a lion's mouth. On the other side of the square are the Roman baths, vestiges of the city's first spa.

   And the Santa Susana hospital, which is now the Thermalia complex, a museum about hot springs. The square has a couple of spas and also restaurants where you can enjoy its beauty quietly sitting on a terrace.

Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui is a parish church in Caldes de Montbui (Vallès Oriental) protected as a cultural asset of local interest. It is in the center of the town of Caldes de Montbui, at the southern end of the historic center, right where the Portal de Barcelona was. In front of the main door of the temple, the Church Square opens, on the north side, Rome Street and the Om Square, and on the east side, Font i Boet Street begins. On the west and south-east side, the church faces a small square above the timba of the Caldes stream.
Large shaded park with picnic tables, fountains, children's area, public toilet, bar, etc. At night the park is closed.
